body:
DEFERRED-WITH-TRIGGER. This spec stays open with empty acceptance_criteria[] until one of the following trigger conditions fires:

(1) Memory count crosses ~few-hundred (current ~50 — comfortable for 10x growth before index overhead becomes load-bearing).
(2) Synonym/stemming false-negatives become noticeable in practice. Conformance audit signal: spec.close blocks where memories_considered does not match a stemmed-equivalent of the topic.
(3) Threshold calibration becomes painful — the current MEMORY_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.3 is a hand-picked guess; BM25 scores are unbounded but normalisable.

DESIGN CONSTRAINT (locked at spec-open time, 2026-05-20): the migration is NOT 'BM25 replaces the hand-rolled ranker.' It is 'BM25 replaces the body component while the label-overlap signal stays parallel.' Rationale: orbit's memories carry labels (drive, topology, code-investigate, etc.) that mark about-ness; bodies that mention a term are a weaker signal than labels that declare topic. BM25 alone flattens this distinction. The right shape is:

  score = bm25_body_norm * body_weight + label_overlap_norm * label_weight

with the existing 2x label-vs-body ratio preserved (or recalibrated as part of the migration).

When the trigger fires, run /orb:design 2026-05-20-memory-match-bm25 to produce interview.md and populate acceptance_criteria. Anticipated AC shape: FTS5 virtual table + tokeniser config + body-only BM25 wrapper + parallel label signal + threshold recalibration + reason-string composition + drift-test fixture (5-7 ACs).
